edges.description: Loss of OAT activity causes elevated plasma ornithine.
edges.source: OAT deficiency and impaired ornithine transamination

edges.description: OAT deficiency is associated with reduced glutamate, lysine, and creatine pools, including low cerebral creatine in some affected patients.
edges.source: OAT deficiency and impaired ornithine transamination
edges.target: Secondary amino acid and creatine depletion

edges.description: Sustained hyperornithinemia drives the retinal degenerative process.
Chronic hyperornithinemia first injures retinal pigment epithelial cells in OAT-deficient mouse retina.
Hyperornithinemia is measured as elevated plasma ornithine.
Ornithine accumulation contributes to cataract as part of the ocular disease.
Ornithine accumulation is associated with childhood myopia as an early ocular feature.
Chorioretinal degeneration leads to early night blindness.
Progressive retinal and choroidal degeneration manifests as chorioretinal atrophy.
Peripheral retinal degeneration causes progressive visual field constriction.
Ongoing retinal degeneration causes progressive visual loss.
Early RPE injury progresses to broader retinal degeneration and photoreceptor cell loss.
Low cerebral creatine may contribute to cognitive impairment in the extraocular disease spectrum.
edges.source: Secondary amino acid and creatine depletion

edges.description: Low cerebral creatine and secondary metabolic imbalance may contribute to muscle weakness in some patients.
edges.source: Secondary amino acid and creatine depletion
Adjunctive lysine, proline, and creatine supplementation
Adjunctive lysine, proline, and creatine supplementation
edges.target: Secondary amino acid and creatine depletion

edges.source: Adjunctive lysine, proline, and creatine supplementation
Adjunctive lysine, proline, and creatine supplementation
Adjunctive lysine, proline, and creatine supplementation

edges.description: Elevated plasma L-ornithine directly reports the hyperornithinemia caused by impaired OAT-mediated ornithine catabolism.
Low cerebral creatine reports the secondary creatine depletion node that may contribute to extraocular neurologic and neuromuscular features.
edges.source: Secondary amino acid and creatine depletion

edges.target: OAT deficiency and impaired ornithine transamination

nodes.description: Lysine, proline, or creatine supplementation may be combined with arginine restriction and pyridoxine to improve metabolic control; lysine has reported ornithine-lowering effects in case-based treatment literature, while creatine supplementation is biologically aligned with reported low cerebral creatine.
nodes.id: Adjunctive lysine, proline, and creatine supplementation

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: An arginine-restricted diet is combined with supplementation with pyridoxine, lysine, proline, or creatine to improve metabolic control.

evidence.reference_title: A review of treatment modalities in gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina (GACR).
evidence.snippet: Based primarily on case reports ornithine levels can be reduced by using a protein restricted diet, pyridoxine supplementation (variation-dependent) and/or lysine supplementation.

nodes.description: Dietary arginine restriction is used to improve metabolic control and lower plasma ornithine burden.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: An arginine-restricted diet is combined with supplementation with pyridoxine, lysine, proline, or creatine to improve metabolic control.

evidence.reference_title: A review of treatment modalities in gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina (GACR).
evidence.snippet: Protein-restricted diets lowered ornithine levels ranging from 16.0-91.2%.

evidence.reference_title: Correction of ornithine accumulation prevents retinal degeneration in a mouse model of gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina.
evidence.snippet: an arginine-restricted diet substantially reduces plasma ornithine levels and completely prevents retinal degeneration in Oat(-/-)

nodes.description: Cataracts are a frequent component of the progressive eye disease.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: Night blindness, cataracts, vision loss, and impaired cognitive functions can be seen.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina: A Review.
evidence.snippet: leads primarily to characteristic ophthalmic manifestations including myopia, cataract, progressive chorioretinal atrophy, and macular changes.

evidence.reference_title: First report of c.425-1G>A mutation in ornithine aminotransferase gene causing gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina with hyperornithinemia.
evidence.snippet: the increased ornithine level is the clinical manifestation of gyrate atrophy of choroid and retina, muscle weakness, moderate mental retardation, and low cerebral creatine.

readouts.interpretation: Low cerebral creatine reports the secondary creatine depletion node that may contribute to extraocular neurologic and neuromuscular features.
readouts.target: Secondary amino acid and creatine depletion

nodes.description: Progressive chorioretinal atrophy is the hallmark ophthalmologic finding, with characteristic patches of atrophy in the peripheral retina extending into the macular area.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina: A Review.
evidence.snippet: leads primarily to characteristic ophthalmic manifestations including myopia, cataract, progressive chorioretinal atrophy, and macular changes.

evidence.reference_title: Deficit of human ornithine aminotransferase in gyrate atrophy: Molecular, cellular, and clinical aspects.
evidence.snippet: Gyrate Atrophy (GA) of the choroid and retina (MIM# 258870) is an autosomal recessive disorder due to mutations of the OAT gene encoding ornithine-delta-aminotransferase (OAT), associated with progressive retinal deterioration and blindness.

nodes.description: Cognitive impairment can be present in some affected individuals alongside the core ophthalmologic manifestations, although most patients have unaffected cognition.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: Night blindness, cataracts, vision loss, and impaired cognitive functions can be seen.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Studies Reveal Folding Defects of Human Ornithine Aminotransferase Variants Associated With Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina.
evidence.snippet: In most GA patients cognition is unaffected

nodes.description: Visual field constriction follows the onset of night blindness as chorioretinal atrophy progresses from the periphery toward the macula.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina: A Review.
evidence.snippet: Patients usually present with night blindness that starts in the first decade of life followed by visual field constriction and eventually diminution of the central visual acuity and blindness.

nodes.description: Markedly elevated plasma ornithine is the defining biochemical hallmark of the disorder, resulting from impaired ornithine catabolism due to OAT deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: All patients had high plasma ornithine levels at the time of diagnosis, and they were all started on an arginine-restricted diet.

evidence.reference_title: Correction of ornithine accumulation prevents retinal degeneration in a mouse model of gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina.
evidence.snippet: Deficiency of ornithine-delta-aminotransferase (OAT) in humans results in gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina (GA), an autosomal recessive disorder characterized by ornithine accumulation and a progressive chorioretinal degeneration of unknown pathogenesis.

nodes.description: Muscle weakness and hypotonia can occur in some patients with gyrate atrophy, associated with low cerebral creatine levels.

evidence.reference_title: First report of c.425-1G>A mutation in ornithine aminotransferase gene causing gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina with hyperornithinemia.
evidence.snippet: the increased ornithine level is the clinical manifestation of gyrate atrophy of choroid and retina, muscle weakness, moderate mental retardation, and low cerebral creatine.

nodes.description: Myopia develops during childhood and is a characteristic early ophthalmic feature of gyrate atrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina: A Review.
evidence.snippet: leads primarily to characteristic ophthalmic manifestations including myopia, cataract, progressive chorioretinal atrophy, and macular changes.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Studies Reveal Folding Defects of Human Ornithine Aminotransferase Variants Associated With Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina.
evidence.snippet: Patients develop myopia during childhood, and show a progressive decrease in visual acuity from the 2° to the 3° decade leading to blindness within the 5° decade

nodes.description: Night blindness is typically the earliest visual symptom, starting in the first decade of life.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: Night blindness, cataracts, vision loss, and impaired cognitive functions can be seen.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina: A Review.
evidence.snippet: Patients usually present with night blindness that starts in the first decade of life followed by visual field constriction and eventually diminution of the central visual acuity and blindness.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Studies Reveal Folding Defects of Human Ornithine Aminotransferase Variants Associated With Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina.
evidence.snippet: The deficit of human ornithine aminotransferase (hOAT) is responsible for gyrate atrophy (GA), a rare recessive inherited disorder.

evidence.reference_title: A review of treatment modalities in gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina (GACR).
evidence.snippet: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina (GACR) is a rare inborn error of amino acid metabolism caused by bi-allelic variations in OAT.

evidence.reference_title: OAT / ornithine aminotransferase deficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: OAT | HGNC:8091 | ornithine aminotransferase deficiency | MONDO:0009796 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: OAT deficiency disrupts mitochondrial matrix, PLP-dependent ornithine transamination, blocking L-ornithine catabolism to glutamate semialdehyde/P5C and glutamate and causing the metabolic defect underlying gyrate atrophy.
nodes.id: OAT deficiency and impaired ornithine transamination

evidence.reference_title: Current Insight into Human Ornithine Aminotransferase: A Review
evidence.snippet: Human ornithine aminotransferase (hOAT) is a mitochondrial matrix pyridoxal‐5′‐phosphate enzyme (PLP) that catalyzes the reversible transfer of the δ‐amino group of L‐ornithine (L‐Orn) to α‐ketoglutarate (α‐KG) yielding glutamate‐5‐semialdehyde (GSA) and glutamate.

evidence.reference_title: Deficit of human ornithine aminotransferase in gyrate atrophy: Molecular, cellular, and clinical aspects.
evidence.snippet: OAT is mainly involved in ornithine catabolism in adults, thus explaining the hyperornithinemia as hallmark of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Studies Reveal Folding Defects of Human Ornithine Aminotransferase Variants Associated With Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina.
evidence.snippet: The deficit of hOAT leads to a 10-to-15 fold increase in the plasmatic L-Orn concentration, along with a small reduction in the levels of glutamate, glutamine, lysine and creatine

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: All patients had high plasma ornithine levels at the time of diagnosis, and they were all started on an arginine-restricted diet.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Studies Reveal Folding Defects of Human Ornithine Aminotransferase Variants Associated With Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina.
evidence.snippet: The deficit of hOAT leads to a 10-to-15 fold increase in the plasmatic L-Orn concentration, along with a small reduction in the levels of glutamate, glutamine, lysine and creatine

readouts.interpretation: Elevated plasma L-ornithine directly reports the hyperornithinemia caused by impaired OAT-mediated ornithine catabolism.

nodes.description: The downstream ophthalmologic disease causes progressive retinal and choroidal damage that drives nyctalopia, visual field constriction, and visual decline.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: The patients primarily present with progressive ophthalmological findings.

evidence.reference_title: Correction of ornithine accumulation prevents retinal degeneration in a mouse model of gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina.
evidence.snippet: an arginine-restricted diet substantially reduces plasma ornithine levels and completely prevents retinal degeneration in Oat(-/-)

evidence.reference_title: A mouse model of gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina. Early retinal pigment epithelium damage and progressive retinal degeneration.
evidence.snippet: Our results indicate that retinal pigment epithelial cells are the initial site of insult in GA and that the OAT-deficient mouse is an excellent animal model of GA in human patients.

nodes.description: Pyridoxine is used as adjunctive therapy in an attempt to improve metabolic control in some patients, although responsiveness is mutation-dependent and not universal. Related supplementation regimens may also include lysine, proline, or creatine in combination with arginine restriction.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: An arginine-restricted diet is combined with supplementation with pyridoxine, lysine, proline, or creatine to improve metabolic control.

evidence.reference_title: A review of treatment modalities in gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina (GACR).
evidence.snippet: Pyridoxine responsiveness was reported in 30% of included mutations.

nodes.description: Retinal pigment epithelial cells are an early site of insult in the hyperornithinemic OAT-deficient retina, preceding progressive photoreceptor disorganization and cell loss.

evidence.reference_title: A mouse model of gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina. Early retinal pigment epithelium damage and progressive retinal degeneration.
evidence.snippet: At 2 mo, the retinal pigment epithelium is histologically normal, but electron microscopy reveals sporadic degeneration of scattered pigment epithelial cells.

evidence.reference_title: A mouse model of gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina. Early retinal pigment epithelium damage and progressive retinal degeneration.
evidence.snippet: Our results indicate that retinal pigment epithelial cells are the initial site of insult in GA and that the OAT-deficient mouse is an excellent animal model of GA in human patients.

nodes.description: The OAT metabolic lesion is accompanied by reduced glutamate, lysine, and creatine pools; low cerebral creatine provides a plausible biochemical link to extraocular neurologic and neuromuscular manifestations in a subset of patients.
nodes.id: Secondary amino acid and creatine depletion

evidence.reference_title: Molecular and Cellular Studies Reveal Folding Defects of Human Ornithine Aminotransferase Variants Associated With Gyrate Atrophy of the Choroid and Retina.
evidence.snippet: The deficit of hOAT leads to a 10-to-15 fold increase in the plasmatic L-Orn concentration, along with a small reduction in the levels of glutamate, glutamine, lysine and creatine

evidence.reference_title: First report of c.425-1G>A mutation in ornithine aminotransferase gene causing gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina with hyperornithinemia.
evidence.snippet: the increased ornithine level is the clinical manifestation of gyrate atrophy of choroid and retina, muscle weakness, moderate mental retardation, and low cerebral creatine.

nodes.description: Progressive retinal degeneration leads to declining visual function over time, ultimately progressing to blindness.

evidence.reference_title: Gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina: a tertiary center experience
evidence.snippet: Night blindness, cataracts, vision loss, and impaired cognitive functions can be seen.

evidence.reference_title: A review of treatment modalities in gyrate atrophy of the choroid and retina (GACR).
evidence.snippet: GACR is characterised by vision decline in early life eventually leading to complete blindness, and high plasma ornithine levels.
